phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: The filterPeer function performs partial ENR field validation. It validates eth2 fork digest (current_fork_digest, next_fork_version, next_fork_epoch) but does NOT validate custody_group_count (cgc) before peer acceptance.

state_anomalies_found.description: cgc field from incoming peer ENR is NOT validated in filterPeer. Peers can be accepted with arbitrary cgc values.
Fork validation bypassed when genesisValidatorsRoot is nil (pre-genesis state).

phase2_symbolic_execution.counterexample_scenario: Attacker-controlled ENR with arbitrary cgc value passes filterPeer validation and is stored in peer database

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Cannot prove cgc validation invariant - no cgc validation exists in filterPeer before peers.Add()
phase3_invariant_proving.recommendation: Add cgc validation in filterPeer() before s.peers.Add(). Check cgc is within valid range (1 to NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS).

rationale: Missing cgc validation allows attacker to advertise arbitrary custody group counts via ENR, potentially affecting PeerDAS data availability sampling.
